question:
A-3. Let \( n \) be a fixed positive integer and let \( b(n) \) be the minimum value of
\[
k+\frac{n}{k}
\]
as \( k \) is allowed to range through all positive integers. Prove that \( b(n) \) and \( \sqrt{4 n+1} \) have the same integer part. [The "integer part" of a real number is the greatest integer which does not exceed it, e.g. for \( \pi \) it is 3 , for \( \sqrt{21} \) it is 4 , for 5 it is 5 , etc.]
solution:
A-3. Let \( c(n)=\sqrt{4 n+1} \) and let \( [x] \) denote the greatest integer in \( x \); then we wish to show that \( [b(n)]=[c(n)] \). Let \( k(n) \) be a value of \( k \) that minimizes \( k+(n / k) \). Then
\[
b(n-1) \leqq k(n)+\{(n-1) / k(n)\}<k(n)+\{n / k(n)\}=b(n)
\]
i.e., \( b(n-1)<b(n) \). Let \( m \) be a positive integer. Then
\[
b\left(m^{2}\right)=2 m, b\left(m^{2}+m\right)=2 m+1 .
\]

It follows from formulas (I) and the strictly increasing nature of \( b(n) \) that
\[
\begin{array}{c}
{[b(n)]=2 m \text { for } m^{2} \leqq n<m^{2}+m,[b(n)]=2 m+1 \text { for } m^{2}+m} \\
\leqq n<(m+1)^{2}
\end{array}
\]

On the other hand, \( c(n) \) is also an increasing function and
\[
\begin{aligned}
c\left(m^{2}-1\right)=\sqrt{4 m^{2}-3}<2 m, c\left(m^{2}\right) & =\sqrt{4 m^{2}+1}>2 m, c\left(m^{2}+m\right) \\
& =\sqrt{4 m^{2}+4 m+1}=2 m+1
\end{aligned}
\]

These facts show that (II) remains true when \( [c(n)] \) is substituted for \( [b(n)] \).

descriptive_long.question:
A-3. Let \( indexvalue \) be a fixed positive integer and let \( minimizer(indexvalue) \) be the minimum value of
\[
divisorvalue+\frac{indexvalue}{divisorvalue}
\]
as \( divisorvalue \) is allowed to range through all positive integers. Prove that \( minimizer(indexvalue) \) and \( \sqrt{4 indexvalue+1} \) have the same integer part. [The "integer part" of a real number is the greatest integer which does not exceed it, e.g. for \( \pi \) it is 3 , for \( \sqrt{21} \) it is 4 , for 5 it is 5 , etc.]
descriptive_long.solution:
A-3. Let \( rootvalue(indexvalue)=\sqrt{4 indexvalue+1} \) and let \([realnumber]\) denote the greatest integer in \( realnumber \); then we wish to show that \([minimizer(indexvalue)]=[rootvalue(indexvalue)]\). Let \( divisorvalue(indexvalue) \) be a value of \( divisorvalue \) that minimizes \( divisorvalue+(indexvalue / divisorvalue) \). Then
\[
minimizer(indexvalue-1) \leqq divisorvalue(indexvalue)+\{(indexvalue-1) / divisorvalue(indexvalue)\}<divisorvalue(indexvalue)+\{indexvalue / divisorvalue(indexvalue)\}=minimizer(indexvalue)
\]
i.e., \( minimizer(indexvalue-1)<minimizer(indexvalue) \). Let \( integerparam \) be a positive integer. Then
\[
minimizer\left(integerparam^{2}\right)=2 integerparam, \quad minimizer\left(integerparam^{2}+integerparam\right)=2 integerparam+1 .
\]
It follows from formulas (I) and the strictly increasing nature of \( minimizer(indexvalue) \) that
\[
\begin{array}{c}
{[minimizer(indexvalue)]=2 integerparam \text { for } integerparam^{2} \leqq indexvalue<integerparam^{2}+integerparam,[minimizer(indexvalue)]=2 integerparam+1 \text { for } integerparam^{2}+integerparam} \\
\leqq indexvalue<(integerparam+1)^{2}
\end{array}
\]
On the other hand, \( rootvalue(indexvalue) \) is also an increasing function and
\[
\begin{aligned}
rootvalue\left(integerparam^{2}-1\right)=\sqrt{4 integerparam^{2}-3}<2 integerparam, \quad rootvalue\left(integerparam^{2}\right) & =\sqrt{4 integerparam^{2}+1}>2 integerparam, \\ rootvalue\left(integerparam^{2}+integerparam\right) & =\sqrt{4 integerparam^{2}+4 integerparam+1}=2 integerparam+1
\end{aligned}
\]
These facts show that (II) remains true when \([rootvalue(indexvalue)]\) is substituted for \([minimizer(indexvalue)]\).

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
A-3. Let \( orchardgoat \) be a fixed positive integer and let \( spectrumleaf(orchardgoat) \) be the minimum value of
\[
lanternmist+\frac{orchardgoat}{lanternmist}
\]
as \( lanternmist \) is allowed to range through all positive integers. Prove that \( spectrumleaf(orchardgoat) \) and \( \sqrt{4 orchardgoat+1} \) have the same integer part. [The "integer part" of a real number is the greatest integer which does not exceed it, e.g. for \( \pi \) it is 3 , for \( \sqrt{21} \) it is 4 , for 5 it is 5 , etc.]
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
A-3. Let \( thunderpatch(orchardgoat)=\sqrt{4 orchardgoat+1} \) and let \( [harborclock] \) denote the greatest integer in \( harborclock \); then we wish to show that \( [spectrumleaf(orchardgoat)]=[thunderpatch(orchardgoat)] \). Let \( lanternmist(orchardgoat) \) be a value of \( lanternmist \) that minimizes \( lanternmist+(orchardgoat / lanternmist) \). Then
\[
spectrumleaf(orchardgoat-1) \leqq lanternmist(orchardgoat)+\{(orchardgoat-1) / lanternmist(orchardgoat)\}<lanternmist(orchardgoat)+\{orchardgoat / lanternmist(orchardgoat)\}=spectrumleaf(orchardgoat)
\]
i.e., \( spectrumleaf(orchardgoat-1)<spectrumleaf(orchardgoat) \). Let \( quiverstone \) be a positive integer. Then
\[
spectrumleaf\left(quiverstone^{2}\right)=2 quiverstone, spectrumleaf\left(quiverstone^{2}+quiverstone\right)=2 quiverstone+1 .
\]

It follows from formulas (I) and the strictly increasing nature of \( spectrumleaf(orchardgoat) \) that
\[
\begin{array}{c}
{[spectrumleaf(orchardgoat)]=2 quiverstone \text { for } quiverstone^{2} \leqq orchardgoat<quiverstone^{2}+quiverstone,[spectrumleaf(orchardgoat)]=2 quiverstone+1 \text { for } quiverstone^{2}+quiverstone} \\
\leqq orchardgoat<(quiverstone+1)^{2}
\end{array}
\]

On the other hand, \( thunderpatch(orchardgoat) \) is also an increasing function and
\[
\begin{aligned}
thunderpatch\left(quiverstone^{2}-1\right)=\sqrt{4 quiverstone^{2}-3}<2 quiverstone, thunderpatch\left(quiverstone^{2}\right) & =\sqrt{4 quiverstone^{2}+1}>2 quiverstone, thunderpatch\left(quiverstone^{2}+quiverstone\right) \\
& =\sqrt{4 quiverstone^{2}+4 quiverstone+1}=2 quiverstone+1
\end{aligned}
\]

These facts show that (II) remains true when \( [thunderpatch(orchardgoat)] \) is substituted for \( [spectrumleaf(orchardgoat)] \).

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
A-3. Let \( irrational \) be a fixed positive integer and let \( upperbound(irrational) \) be the minimum value of
\[
continuous+\frac{irrational}{continuous}
\]
as \( continuous \) is allowed to range through all positive integers. Prove that \( upperbound(irrational) \) and \( \sqrt{4 irrational+1} \) have the same integer part. [The "integer part" of a real number is the greatest integer which does not exceed it, e.g. for \( \pi \) it is 3 , for \( \sqrt{21} \) it is 4 , for 5 it is 5 , etc.]
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
A-3. Let \( smallest(irrational)=\sqrt{4 irrational+1} \) and let \( [knownvalue] \) denote the greatest integer in \( knownvalue \); then we wish to show that \( [upperbound(irrational)]=[smallest(irrational)] \). Let \( continuous(irrational) \) be a value of \( continuous \) that minimizes \( continuous+(irrational / continuous) \). Then
\[
upperbound(irrational-1) \leqq continuous(irrational)+\{(irrational-1) / continuous(irrational)\}<continuous(irrational)+\{irrational / continuous(irrational)\}=upperbound(irrational)
\]
i.e., \( upperbound(irrational-1)<upperbound(irrational) \). Let \( noninteger \) be a positive integer. Then
\[
upperbound\left(noninteger^{2}\right)=2 noninteger, upperbound\left(noninteger^{2}+noninteger\right)=2 noninteger+1 .
\]

It follows from formulas (I) and the strictly increasing nature of \( upperbound(irrational) \) that
\[
\begin{array}{c}
{[upperbound(irrational)]=2 noninteger \text { for } noninteger^{2} \leqq irrational<noninteger^{2}+noninteger,[upperbound(irrational)]=2 noninteger+1 \text { for } noninteger^{2}+noninteger} \\
\leqq irrational<(noninteger+1)^{2}
\end{array}
\]

On the other hand, \( smallest(irrational) \) is also an increasing function and
\[
\begin{aligned}
smallest\left(noninteger^{2}-1\right)=\sqrt{4 noninteger^{2}-3}<2 noninteger, smallest\left(noninteger^{2}\right) & =\sqrt{4 noninteger^{2}+1}>2 noninteger, smallest\left(noninteger^{2}+noninteger\right) \\
& =\sqrt{4 noninteger^{2}+4 noninteger+1}=2 noninteger+1
\end{aligned}
\]

These facts show that (II) remains true when \( [smallest(irrational)] \) is substituted for \( [upperbound(irrational)] \).

garbled_string.question:
A-3. Let \( zqtrblmn \) be a fixed positive integer and let \( pqwelyui(zqtrblmn) \) be the minimum value of
\[
hvcxroje+\frac{zqtrblmn}{hvcxroje}
\]
as \( hvcxroje \) is allowed to range through all positive integers. Prove that \( pqwelyui(zqtrblmn) \) and \( \sqrt{4 zqtrblmn+1} \) have the same integer part. [The "integer part" of a real number is the greatest integer which does not exceed it, e.g. for \( \pi \) it is 3 , for \( \sqrt{21} \) it is 4 , for 5 it is 5 , etc.]
garbled_string.solution:
A-3. Let \( mnbvdser(zqtrblmn)=\sqrt{4 zqtrblmn+1} \) and let \( [gfnesvot] \) denote the greatest integer in \( gfnesvot \); then we wish to show that \( [pqwelyui(zqtrblmn)]=[mnbvdser(zqtrblmn)] \). Let \( hvcxroje(zqtrblmn) \) be a value of \( hvcxroje \) that minimizes \( hvcxroje+(zqtrblmn / hvcxroje) \). Then
\[
pqwelyui(zqtrblmn-1) \leqq hvcxroje(zqtrblmn)+\{(zqtrblmn-1) / hvcxroje(zqtrblmn)\}<hvcxroje(zqtrblmn)+\{zqtrblmn / hvcxroje(zqtrblmn)\}=pqwelyui(zqtrblmn)
\]
i.e., \( pqwelyui(zqtrblmn-1)<pqwelyui(zqtrblmn) \). Let \( dlkiuqpa \) be a positive integer. Then
\[
pqwelyui\left(dlkiuqpa^{2}\right)=2 dlkiuqpa, \quad pqwelyui\left(dlkiuqpa^{2}+dlkiuqpa\right)=2 dlkiuqpa+1 .
\]

It follows from formulas (I) and the strictly increasing nature of \( pqwelyui(zqtrblmn) \) that
\[
\begin{array}{c}
{[pqwelyui(zqtrblmn)]=2 dlkiuqpa \text { for } dlkiuqpa^{2} \leqq zqtrblmn<dlkiuqpa^{2}+dlkiuqpa,[pqwelyui(zqtrblmn)]=2 dlkiuqpa+1 \text { for } dlkiuqpa^{2}+dlkiuqpa} \\
\leqq zqtrblmn<(dlkiuqpa+1)^{2}
\end{array}
\]

On the other hand, \( mnbvdser(zqtrblmn) \) is also an increasing function and
\[
\begin{aligned}
mnbvdser\left(dlkiuqpa^{2}-1\right)=\sqrt{4 dlkiuqpa^{2}-3}<2 dlkiuqpa, \quad mnbvdser\left(dlkiuqpa^{2}\right) & =\sqrt{4 dlkiuqpa^{2}+1}>2 dlkiuqpa, \\ mnbvdser\left(dlkiuqpa^{2}+dlkiuqpa\right) & =\sqrt{4 dlkiuqpa^{2}+4 dlkiuqpa+1}=2 dlkiuqpa+1
\end{aligned}
\]

These facts show that (II) remains true when \( [mnbvdser(zqtrblmn)] \) is substituted for \( [pqwelyui(zqtrblmn)] \).

kernel_variant.question:
For a positive integer n define  
 b(n)=min_{k\in \mathbb{Z}_{>0}} (k+n/k) and M(n)={k>0 : k+n/k=b(n)}.  

(a) Prove that \lfloor b(n)\rfloor =\lfloor \sqrt{4n+1}\rfloor .  
(b) Determine the complete set M(n).  
(c) Show the sharp estimate 0\leq b(n)-2\sqrt{n}<1/(2\sqrt{n}).
kernel_variant.solution:
Write f_n(k)=k+n/k, c(n)=\sqrt{4n+1}, and let m=\lfloor \sqrt{n}\rfloor .

1.  Monotonicity. For every k, f_{n+1}(k)=f_n(k)+1/k>f_n(k); hence b(n+1)>b(n).

2.  Anchor values. By AM-GM, f_{m^2}(m)=2m is minimal, so b(m^2)=2m.  
 Similarly f_{m^2+m}(m)=f_{m^2+m}(m+1)=2m+1 and neighbouring k's give larger
 values, whence b(m^2+m)=2m+1.  Using the strict increase proved in 1,

  m^2\leq n<m^2+m \Rightarrow  2m\leq b(n)<2m+1,  
  m^2+m\leq n<(m+1)^2 \Rightarrow  2m+1\leq b(n)<2m+2.

 Taking integer parts yields (a): \lfloor b(n)\rfloor =\lfloor c(n)\rfloor .

3.  Minimizers.  Because k\mapsto f_n(k) is convex, a minimiser lies in {m,m+1}.  
 Direct comparison gives  
  M(n)={m}  if m^2\leq n<m^2+m;  
  M(n)={m,m+1} if n=m^2+m;  
  M(n)={m+1} if m^2+m<n<(m+1)^2.

4.  Error bound.  Write f_n(k)=2\sqrt{n}+(k-\sqrt{n})^2/k.  
 For k\in M(n) we have |k-\sqrt{n}|\leq \frac{1}{2}, hence

  0\leq b(n)-2\sqrt{n}<(\frac{1}{2})^2/(\sqrt{n}-\frac{1}{2})<1/(2\sqrt{n}),

 establishing (c).

_replacement_note.reason: Original kernel variant was too easy compared to the original problem
